data.text: I’ve read the cache implementation and the current test coverage. Next I’m checking whether the two flagged methods are genuinely unused, or whether they are only unreferenced from the deadcode root because the surrounding cache path itself is already stranded.

The current `cache.go` doesn’t match the symbol names in the violation file, which means the initial audit was against an earlier revision of the package. I’m lining up the current file with the recorded evidence so I can decide whether the stale methods have already been removed or if the deadcode report just needs to be re-run after the current state is fixed.

data.text: `go test ./...` is taking longer than usual in this tree. I’m letting it finish rather than guessing, then I’ll run the deadcode check against `cli/` to confirm the exact production-reachability state before deciding whether any code changes are needed.

The test suite is green, so the remaining question is pure reachability evidence. I’m running the exact deadcode RTA command from `cli/` now; if it comes back clean, I’ll record the symbol decisions and finish with a single commit for the evidence artifact.
